LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: CROSS DEFENDANT SHARMAN SPECTOR'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 21, 2014 (PER THE PARTIES AGREEMENT) TO GIVE THE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6C. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPERATIVE PLEADING THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF THE MOTION TO STRIKE TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 21, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING, OPPOSITION AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-24-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF MAR-12-2014 CONTINUED TO MAY-07-2014 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT SHARMAN SPECTOR'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CLAIM FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES. THE CROSS-COMPLAINANTS FAIL TO CITE A STATUTORY OR CONTRACTUAL BASIS FOR THE CLAIM. THE REMAINDER OF THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT AS A WHOLE HAS SUFFICIENT ALLEGATIONS REGARDING THE SPECIAL DAMAGES. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT PLEADS SUFFICIENT FACTS REGARDING CROSS-DEFENDANT'S BREACHES OF FIDUCIARY DUTY THAT SUPPORT THE CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. AT 11:15-20, THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ALLEGES PHYSICAL INJURY AND WHILE THE RECOVERY OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS DAMAGES IS DUBIOUS, IT IS PREMATURE TO STRIKE THE CLAIM. THE COURT DENIES CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED. (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT SHARMAN SPECTOR'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS HAVE LEAVE TO ATTACH A COPY OF THE CONTRACT/S, PLEAD THEIR TERMS IN HAEC VERBA, OR PLEAD THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT/S ACCORDING TO THEIR LEGAL EFFECT. IN ADDITION, IF CROSS-COMPLAINANTS SEEK TO ALLEGE THE BREACH OF MULTIPLE CONTRACTS, EACH CONTRACT SUPPORTS A DISTINCT CAUSE OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD THE CLAIM WITH MORE SPECIFICITY. CROSS-DEFENDANT DID NOT DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY. THE DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION IS OVERRULED. IN PCO, INC. V. CHRISTENSEN, MILLER, FINK, JACOB, ET AL. (2007) 150 CAL.APP.4TH 384, 396, THE COURT EXPLAINS: CALIFORNIA CASES PERMITTING AN ACTION FOR CONVERSION OF MONEY TYPICALLY INVOLVE THOSE WHO HAVE MISAPPROPRIATED, COMMINGLED, OR MISAPPLIED SPECIFIC FUNDS HELD FOR THE BENEFIT OF OTHERS. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO CLARIFY EXACTLY WHAT PORTIONS OF THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT/S THE CROSS-COMPLAINANTS SEEK A JUDICIAL DETERMINATION REGARDING. THE DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE THE CLAIM FOR AN INJUNCTION AS A REMEDY/PRAYER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED. (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT SHARMAN SPECTOR'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT OF BEVERLY SPECTOR AND AUDREY SPECTOR- THE DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1-3 [BREACH OF CONTRACT] ARE OVERRULED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACTS ACCORDING TO THEIR LEGAL INTENDMENT AND EFFECT. CROSS-COMPLAINANT ALLEGES THE SUBSTANCE OF THE KEY TERMS OF EACH CONTRACT AND IS NOT OBLIGED TO ATTACH A COPY OF THE WRITTEN AGREEMENTS TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER TO THE FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS LEAVE TO PLEAD THE ELEMENTS OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION (SEE CACI 1902) WITH MORE PARTICULARITY. THE DEMURRER TO THE BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AND DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSES OF ACTION ARE OVERRULED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: JOHN STEWART; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR #11185, TEL. NO. (951)764-4047 =302/JS

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS-DEFENDANT SHARMAN SPECTOR's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT OF BEVERLY SPECTOR AND AUDREY SPECTOR- The demurrer to causes of action 1-3 [breach of contract] are overruled. Cross-complainant sufficiently alleges the terms of the contracts according to their legal intendment and effect. Cross-complainant alleges the substance of the key terms of each contract and is not obliged to attach a copy of the written agreements to the cross-complaint. The demurrer to the fraud cause of action is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Cross-complainant has leave to plead the elements of the cause of action (see CACI 1902) with more particularity. The demurrer to the breach of fiduciary duty and declaratory relief causes of action are overruled. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: John Stewart; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185, TEL. NO. (951)764-4047 =302/JS

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT SHARMAN SPECTOR'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is granted without leave to amend as to the claim for attorney's fees. The cross-complainants fail to cite a statutory or contractual basis for the claim. The remainder of the motion is denied. The cross-complaint as a whole has sufficient allegations regarding the special damages. The cross-complaint pleads sufficient facts regarding cross-defendant's breaches of fiduciary duty that support the claim for punitive damages. At 11:15-20, the cross-complaint alleges physical injury and while the recovery of emotional distress damages is dubious, it is premature to strike the claim. The Court denies cross-complainant's request for judicial notice. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT SHARMAN SPECTOR'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of contract. Cross-complainants have leave to attach a copy of the contract/s, plead their terms in haec verba, or plead the terms of the contract/s according to their legal effect. In addition, if cross-complainants seek to allege the breach of multiple contracts, each contract supports a distinct cause of action. The demurrer to the second cause of action for fraud is sustained with leave to amend to plead the claim with more specificity. Cross-defendant did not demurrer to the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for conversion is overruled. In PCO, Inc. v. Christensen, Miller, Fink, Jacob, et al. (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 384, 396, the court explains: California cases permitting an action for conversion of money typically involve those who have misappropriated, commingled, or misapplied specific funds held for the benefit of others. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained with leave to amend to clarify exactly what portions of the parties' agreement/s the cross-complainants seek a judicial determination regarding. The demurrer to the sixth cause of action for injunctive relief is sustained with leave to amend to allege the claim for an injunction as a remedy/prayer. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
